title: कामाख्या कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री कामाख्या कवचम् ॥

अस्य श्री कामाख्या कवच मन्त्रस्य भैरव ऋषिः,
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः, श्री कामाख्या देवता,
क्लीं बीजम्, ह्रीं शक्तिः, श्रीं कीलकम्,
सर्वाभीष्टसिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः।

ॐ कामाख्या मे शिरः पातु ललाटं कामरूपिणी।
भ्रूमध्यं पातु कामेशी नेत्रे मे कामदायिनी॥१॥

कर्णौ पातु महामाया नासिकां योगिनीश्वरी।
ओष्ठौ पातु महाविद्या जिह्वां मे सर्वसिद्धिदा॥२॥

कण्ठं मे पातु चामुण्डा स्कन्धौ नीलसरस्वती।
भुजौ पातु महाकाली करौ मे भैरवीश्वरी॥३॥

हृदयं पातु कालिका स्तनौ त्रिपुरसुन्दरी।
उदरं पातु भुवनेश्वरी नाभिं च तारिणी॥४॥

कटिं पातु महालक्ष्मी गुह्यं मे छिन्नमस्तका।
ऊरू पातु मातंगी जानुनी बगला तथा॥५॥

जंघे पातु धूमावती पादौ कमलात्मिका।
सर्वाङ्गं पातु कामाख्या देवी शक्तिः सनातनी॥६॥

इदं कवचमज्ञात्वा यो जपेत् कामरूपिणीम्।
न तस्य जायते सिद्धिः कल्पकोटिशतैरपि॥७॥

कवचं धारयेद्यस्तु कामाख्याभक्तिसंयुतः।
त्रैलोक्ये तस्य किं वश्यं किं तस्यापि दुर्लभम्॥८॥

॥ इति श्री कामाख्या कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Kamakhya Kavacham ||

Asya Shri Kamakhya Kavacha Mantrasya Bhairava Rishah,
Anushtup Chhandah, Shri Kamakhya Devata,
Klim Bijam, Hrim Shaktih, Shrim Kilakam,
Sarva-abhishta-siddhyarthe Jape Viniyogah.

Om Kamakhya Me Shirah Patu Lalatam Kama-rupini.
Bhru-madhyam Patu Kameshi Netre Me Kama-dayini.||1||

Karnau Patu Maha-maya Nasikam Yogini-ishvari.
Oshthau Patu Maha-vidya Jihvam Me Sarva-siddhida.||2||

Kantham Me Patu Chamunda Skandhau Nila-sarasvati.
Bhujau Patu Maha-kali Karau Me Bhairavi-ishvari.||3||

Hridayam Patu Kalika Stanau Tripura-sundari.
Udaram Patu Bhuvaneshvari Nabhim Cha Tarini.||4||

Katim Patu Maha-lakshmi Guhyam Me Chhinnamastaka.
Uru Patu Matangi Januni Bagala Tatha.||5||

Janghe Patu Dhumavati Padau Kamala-atmika.
Sarvangam Patu Kamakhya Devi Shaktih Sanatani.||6||

Idam Kavacham-ajnatva Yo Japet Kama-rupinim.
Na Tasya Jayate Siddhih Kalpa-koti-shataih Api.||7||

Kavacham Dharayed-yastu Kamakhya-bhakti-samyutah.
Trailokye Tasya Kim Vashyam Kim Tasya-api Durlabham.||8||

|| Iti Shri Kamakhya Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: कामाख्या शिर की, कामरूपिणी ललाट की, कामेशी भ्रूमध्य की, कामदायिनी नेत्रों की, महामाया कानों की, योगिनीश्वरी नासिका की, महाविद्या ओंठों की, सर्वसिद्धिदा जिह्वा की, चामुण्डा कण्ठ की, नीलसरस्वती स्कन्धों की, महाकाली भुजाओं की रक्षा करें। इस कवच को जाने बिना जप करने से सिद्धि नहीं होती। कामाख्या भक्ति सहित कवच धारण करने वाले के लिए त्रैलोक्य में कुछ भी दुर्लभ नहीं।
benefits: Protective armor invoking Kamakhya through the ten Mahavidyas
Each Mahavidya protects a specific part of the body
Essential for Kamakhya mantra siddhi and Tantric sadhana
Nothing in the three worlds remains unattainable for the wearer
Powerful Shakti kavacham from the Kalika Purana tradition
description: Kamakhya Kavacham (कामाख्या कवचम्) is a divine armor prayer for protection dedicated to Goddess Durga. Drawn from Kalika Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to essential for Kamakhya mantra siddhi and Tantric sadhana, powerful Shakti kavacham from the Kalika Purana tradition, and nothing in the three worlds remains unattainable for the wearer. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar), Friday (Shukravar), and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Navratri and Ambubachi for maximum spiritual benefit. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Kamakhya Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Kamakhya Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Protective armor invoking Goddess Kamakhya through all ten Mahavidyas for complete protection.
relatedSearches: Kamakhya Kavacham Sanskrit lyrics with Hindi meaning
Kamakhya Devi Kalika Purana origin reference
Kamakhya Shakti Peeth Nilachal Hill Guwahati Assam
Kamakhya Kavach for protection from black magic tantra
Kamakhya Kavach benefits during Ambubachi mela
Kamakhya Devi Yoni Mandala worship significance
Kamakhya Kavach for fertility and progeny blessings

seoFaqs.question: What is the scriptural source of the Kamakhya Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kamakhya Kavacham is rooted in the Kalika Purana, a 10th-century Shakta text considered one of the foundational scriptures of Tantric Devi-worship in Eastern India. Within the Kalika Purana, Kamakhya is identified as the supreme form of the Goddess and is given an extensive mahatmya, ritual prescription and several protective stotras and kavachas. The Yogini Tantra, composed several centuries later in Assam, expands on the Kalika Purana material and includes additional Kamakhya kavachas and dhyana verses that are also recited today. Different lineages preserve slightly different recensions, but the Kalika Purana version is treated as the textual root. (Sources: Wikipedia Kamakhya Temple; amitkumarjha.com; gurukripa.org.in.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Kamakhya Temple considered the most important Shakti Peetha for Kavach recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Kamakhya is held to be the spot where the yoni of Sati fell when Shiva carried her body across the cosmos after the Daksha-yajna, making it the most concentrated centre of feminine creative shakti among all 51 (or 108 in Tantric counts) Shakti Peethas. Unlike most Devi temples, Kamakhya has no anthropomorphic murti; the object of worship is a natural rock cleft fed by an underground spring, identified as the Yoni-mandala. Tantric tradition holds that kavachas dedicated to Kamakhya draw on the raw shakti of this living Peetha, making them especially potent for protection, fertility and removal of obstacles. The annual Ambubachi Mela in June, when the Goddess is believed to menstruate, draws Tantriks from across India for this reason. (Sources: kamakhya.org; templeconnect.com; rudraksha-ratna.com.)

seoFaqs.question: Does the Kamakhya Kavacham require Tantric initiation to recite safely?
seoFaqs.answer: Practice varies by lineage. The bija-mantra-laden full Kamakhya Kavach as used in Vama-marga Tantric sadhana traditionally requires diksha from a qualified guru in the Kamakhya parampara, because it embeds bija-mantras of the Dasha Mahavidyas and specific nyasa procedures that are not meant for uninitiated practice. However, the simpler stotric form of the Kavach published in popular Devi-stotra collections (without the bijas and complex nyasa) is treated as a Puranic prayer and is freely recited by householders, particularly during Navratri and Ambubachi. As a general rule, devotees beginning practice are advised to use the published stotric form and approach Kamakhya through the Devi Mahatmya Kavacham first. (Sources: gurukripa.org.in; rudrakshahub.com; drkkdebnath.blogspot.com.)

seoFaqs.question: What specific protections does Kamakhya Kavach claim that distinguish it from other Devi Kavachas?
seoFaqs.answer: Kamakhya Kavacham is particularly invoked for protection against Tantric attacks (abhichara), black magic, possession by negative entities (graha-badha), and incurable or chronic diseases that have not responded to ordinary treatment. It is also extensively used for fertility, removal of progeny-related obstacles, and difficulties in marriage, drawing on the Yoni-mandala association of Kamakhya as the source of all generation. Unlike the more general Saptashati Devi Kavacham which protects all aspects of life equally, Kamakhya Kavach is sought out for these specific Tantric and reproductive concerns. The text is also recited for siddhi in mantra-sadhana, particularly by practitioners of the Dasha Mahavidya who treat Kamakhya as the unifying source of all ten Mahavidyas. (Sources: gurukripa.org.in; rudraksha-ratna.com; kamakhya.org Dashamahavidyas page.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the most auspicious time to recite Kamakhya Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The most powerful occasions are the four days of Ambubachi Mela in mid-June, when the temple is closed and the Goddess is held to be in menstrual seclusion; recitation during this period (outside the temple, since darshan is not available) is believed to align the sadhaka with the peak of Kamakhya's annual shakti cycle. Other potent times include Manasa Ashtami, Kali Chaudas, every Tuesday and Friday, the eighth and ninth tithis of both Navratris, and Mangala-doshic transits when protection from Mars-related afflictions is sought. Sadhakas of the Vama-marga school also recite during the brahma-muhurta of every Krishna Chaturdashi and amavasya, considered the prime windows for Devi-tantra. (Sources: rudrakshahub.com; templeconnect.com; mystreal.com.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Kamakhya Kavach connected to the Dasha Mahavidya worship at the Nilachal complex?
seoFaqs.answer: The Nilachal Hill complex houses individual shrines for all ten Mahavidyas (Kali, Tara, Tripura Sundari, Bhuvaneshvari, Bhairavi, Chhinnamasta, Dhumavati, Bagalamukhi, Matangi, Kamala) clustered around the central Kamakhya shrine, making it the only site in India where all ten Mahavidyas are worshipped together as living deities. The Kamakhya Kavacham reflects this by including invocations to all ten Mahavidyas as the protective limbs of Kamakhya herself, treating Kamakhya as the unified mula-shakti from whom the ten emanate. Devotees performing the Kavach as part of a Dasha Mahavidya sadhana traditionally complete the practice with a parikrama of all ten shrines on the hill. (Sources: kamakhya.org Dashamahavidyas; amitkumarjha.com; templeconnect.com.)
